answer.content:
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Prim's Algorithm Visualization</title>
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: center;
            height: 100vh;
            margin: 0;
        }
        canvas {
            border: 1px solid #000;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>

<canvas id="canvas" width="600" height="600"></canvas>
<button id="startBtn">Start Prim's Algorithm</button>
<script>
const canvas = document.getElementById('canvas');
const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
const vertices = [];
const edges = [];
const numVertices = 10;
const radius = 20;
const speed = 1000;

function randomPosition() {
    return {
        x: Math.random() * (canvas.width - 2 * radius) + radius,
        y: Math.random() * (canvas.height - 2 * radius) + radius
    };
}

function createGraph() {
    // Generate vertices
    for (let i = 0; i < numVertices; i++) {
        const pos = randomPosition();
        vertices.push({ id: i, ...pos });
    }

    // Create edges (randomly connecting vertices with weights)
    for (let i = 0; i < vertices.length; i++) {
        for (let j = i + 1; j < vertices.length; j++) {
            const weight = Math.random() * 10 + 1; // Random weight between 1 and 10
            edges.push({ from: vertices[i], to: vertices[j], weight });
        }
    }
}

function drawGraph() {
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
    
    // Draw edges
    for (const edge of edges) {
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.moveTo(edge.from.x, edge.from.y);
        ctx.lineTo(edge.to.x, edge.to.y);
        ctx.strokeStyle = '#ddd';
        ctx.stroke();
    }

    // Draw vertices
    for (const vertex of vertices) {
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.arc(vertex.x, vertex.y, radius, 0, Math.PI * 2);
        ctx.fillStyle = 'lightblue';
        ctx.fill();
        ctx.stroke();
        ctx.fillStyle = 'black';
        ctx.fillText(vertex.id, vertex.x - 5, vertex.y + 5);
    }
}

function primsAlgorithm() {
    const startVertex = vertices[0];
    const visited = new Set();
    const mstEdges = [];
    
    const minHeap = [{ vertex: startVertex, weight: 0 }];
    let totalWeight = 0;

    function visit(vertex) {
        // Mark vertex as visited
        visited.add(vertex.id);
        ctx.fillStyle = 'yellow';
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.arc(vertex.x, vertex.y, radius, 0, Math.PI * 2);
        ctx.fill();

        for (const edge of edges) {
            if (
                (edge.from.id === vertex.id && !visited.has(edge.to.id)) ||
                (edge.to.id === vertex.id && !visited.has(edge.from.id))
            ) {
                minHeap.push({
                    vertex: edge.to.id === vertex.id ? edge.from : edge.to,
                    weight: edge.weight
                });
            }
        }
    }

    (function run() {
        if (minHeap.length === 0) {
            console.log('MST:', mstEdges);
            return;
        }

        // Extract the edge with minimum weight
        minHeap.sort((a, b) => a.weight - b.weight);
        const { vertex, weight } = minHeap.shift();
        if (visited.has(vertex.id)) return;

        visit(vertex);
        mstEdges.push({ from: startVertex, to: vertex, weight });
        totalWeight += weight;

        setTimeout(run, speed);
    })();
}

// Button click event
document.getElementById('startBtn').onclick = function() {
    createGraph();
    drawGraph();
    primsAlgorithm();
};

</script>

</body>
</html>

messages.content: Generate a single HTML file with JavaScript demonstrating the concept Prim's Algorithm. Only respond in a single HTML file.
